# Honeypot Med

Honeypot Med is an open-source, local-first prompt-injection honeypot for healthcare AI workflows.

## Primary pages
- Home: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/
- FAQ: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/faq/
- Challenge mode: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/challenge/
- Daily Dungeon: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/daily/
- Prompt CTF: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/ctf/
- Casebook: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/casebook/
- Specimen Codex: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/codex/
- Field Notes: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/field-notes/
- Evidence gallery: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/gallery/
- Generated report gallery: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/reports/
- Sample challenge report: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/reports/healthcare-challenge/
- Launch Room: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/launch-room/
- Integrations: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/integrations/
- HF Lab: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/hf-lab/
- Contributor quests: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/contribute/
- Media kit: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/media/
- Releases and installers: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/releases/
- Healthcare AI use case: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/use-cases/healthcare-ai/
- Claims automation use case: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/use-cases/claims-automation/
- Prior authorization use case: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/use-cases/prior-authorization/
- Patient triage use case: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/use-cases/patient-triage/
- Guardrails vs honeypots comparison: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/compare/prompt-guardrails-vs-honeypots/
- Guardrails vs launch review: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/compare/guardrails-vs-launch-review/
- Evals vs proof bundles: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/compare/evals-vs-proof-bundles/
- Honeypot Med vs generic red-team reports: https://byteworthyllc.github.io/honeypot-med/compare/honeypot-med-vs-generic-red-team-report/
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/ByteWorthyLLC/honeypot-med

## Summary
Honeypot Med inspects suspicious prompts and turns the result into human-readable evidence bundles, including challenge scores, daily dungeons, prompt CTF flags, redacted casebooks, traparium pages, README badges, HTML proof pages, PDF summaries, social cards, SARIF reports, JUnit XML, GitHub step summaries, OpenTelemetry log payloads, OpenInference traces, LangSmith JSONL, Hugging Face-ready cards, specimen codex files, trap ledgers, field guides, inquiry notebooks, research-question JSON, unknown ledgers, counterfactual prompt decks, ablation ladders, experiment plans, eval-kit adapters, offline proof files, JSON reports, Markdown summaries, launch-kit copy, and a public installer surface.

## Key facts
- Local-first by default
- No API keys required for normal usage
- Challenge mode asks: "Can your healthcare AI survive 10 traps?"
- Daily Dungeon generates deterministic seeded trap routes from bundled packs
- Prompt CTF uses evidence predicates instead of secret strings
- Casebook mode generates `casebook.html`, `traparium.html`, `unknowns.html`, `failure-recipes.md`, `trap-tree.svg`, and `lab-notebook.ipynb`
- Specimen Codex names failure modes as Compliance Mimic, Roster Leech, Policy Poltergeist, and Quiet Chart Ghost
- Field Notes shifts the project stance toward intellectual curiosity over promotion
- Default challenge output includes an 8/10 survived sample report, badge, SARIF file, OTEL logs, specimen codex, trap ledger, field guide, inquiry notebook, unknown ledger, experiment plan, promptfoo config, Inspect AI dataset, OpenAI Evals adapter, offline proof, JSON, Markdown, PDF, and social card
- Root `action.yml` lets GitHub projects run Honeypot Med in CI and upload artifacts
- Public releases and bootstrap scripts are free and GitHub-native
- Focused on healthcare AI workflows such as claims, prior authorization, intake, triage, appeals, eligibility, and utilization management
- Public site includes FAQ, challenge, daily dungeon, prompt CTF, casebook, specimen codex, field notes, generated reports, launch room, integrations, HF Lab, contributor quests, evidence gallery, release, media, use-case, and comparison surfaces for search and answer-engine discovery
